evidence: QueryExecutor.findCollocations uses 'headword' for the head lemma parameter; executeSurfacePattern uses 'lemma' for the same concept — inconsistent naming for the same domain concept across methods in the same interface

executeSurfacePattern has 6 parameters: (String lemma, String bcqlPattern, int headPosition, int collocatePosition, double minLogDice, int maxResults) — at the upper boundary for an interface method; the ExploreOptions record was created specifically to solve this for SemanticFieldExplorer callers but the interface method itself was not updated

BlackLabQueryExecutor.findDependencyCollocations is a public method not declared on the QueryExecutor interface — it creates an undocumented surface that bypasses the interface contract and cannot be mocked via the interface in tests

buildAndRankCollocates accepts a nullable Map<String, String> posMap — a null-or-map parameter signals that the method is doing two slightly different things, an overloaded behavior hidden as a nullable argument

QueryExecutor.executeQuery uses 'cqlPattern' parameter; executeBcqlQuery uses 'bcqlPattern' — different prefixes for what the interface javadoc acknowledges are two different parsers, which is correct, but callers must read the docs carefully to understand the difference
LogDiceCalculator has two overloads of compute() (double-signature and long-signature) that are consistent and well-designed
The ExploreOptions record in SemanticFieldExplorer is good API design — bundles 8 tuning parameters into a named record, avoiding a >5-parameter call site
The HTTP REST surface is consistent: all read operations are GET, write/execute is POST (/api/bcql, /api/visual/radial); CORS and OPTIONS are handled uniformly via registerGetHandler

strengths: The QueryExecutor interface has thorough Javadoc that documents the distinction between executeQuery (CQL) and executeBcqlQuery (BCQL) and between findCollocations (no position hints) and executeSurfacePattern (position hints) — the intent is clearly communicated
The HTTP REST surface is consistent: GETs for reads, POSTs for execute/render, OPTIONS/CORS handled uniformly
ExploreOptions record successfully prevents a >8-parameter method call at the SemanticFieldExplorer level
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: Inconsistency is concentrated at the QueryExecutor interface boundary: the head-lemma parameter has two names (headword vs lemma) across sibling methods, executeSurfacePattern has 6 parameters, and a public implementation method (findDependencyCollocations) extends beyond the interface without documentation.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The HTTP layer is coherent and the high-level SemanticFieldExplorer API uses good parameter bundling. However, the QueryExecutor interface — the central contract that all components depend on — has inconsistent parameter naming for the same concept, a 6-parameter method that could be bundled, and a public implementation method not declared on the interface. These defects affect every caller of the query layer. A codebase at 75 would have consistent parameter naming across sibling interface methods; at 80 it would also have no undocumented public methods extending the interface in implementations.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor interface uses 'headword' in findCollocations and 'lemma' in executeSurfacePattern for the same concept — inconsistent parameter naming across sibling interface methods
evidence: QueryExecutor.findCollocations signature: findCollocations(String headword, String cqlPattern, double minLogDice, int maxResults)
QueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern signature: executeSurfacePattern(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, int headPosition, int collocatePosition, double minLogDice, int maxResults)
BlackLabQueryExecutor.findDependencyCollocations also uses 'headword' — three slightly different names (headword, lemma, headword) for the same concept across the same class
issues.suggestion: Standardize on 'lemma' throughout the QueryExecutor interface and BlackLabQueryExecutor implementation, since the codebase consistently uses 'lemma' elsewhere (LongIntHashMap key is lemma, GrammarConfigLoader refers to lemmas, LogDiceCalculator docs say lemma).

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.findDependencyCollocations is a public method that does not appear on the QueryExecutor interface, creating an undocumented API extension that bypasses the contract
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor has: public List<QueryResults.WordSketchResult> findDependencyCollocations(String headword, String deprel, double minLogDice, int maxResults) throws IOException
QueryExecutor interface does not declare findDependencyCollocations

WordSketchApiServer uses executor (typed as QueryExecutor) — to call findDependencyCollocations it must cast to BlackLabQueryExecutor, breaking the interface abstraction (or it cannot call it at all through the interface)
issues.suggestion: Add findDependencyCollocations to the QueryExecutor interface with appropriate Javadoc, or make the method package-private if it is only called within the query package.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern has 6 parameters; the ExploreOptions record pattern already in use for SemanticFieldExplorer should be applied here
evidence: executeSurfacePattern(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, int headPosition, int collocatePosition, double minLogDice, int maxResults) — 6 parameters

SemanticFieldExplorer already has ExploreOptions record bundling headPos, collocatePos, topCollocates, minLogDice, minShared, nounsPerCollocate — demonstrating the codebase is aware of the >5-parameter problem
Callers in WordSketchApiServer and SemanticFieldExplorer must pass headPosition and collocatePosition as separate ints, which are positionally adjacent and easy to swap silently
issues.suggestion: Define a SurfacePatternOptions record (or reuse ExploreOptions fields) bundling headPosition, collocatePosition, minLogDice, and maxResults; update the interface method to accept (String lemma, String bcqlPattern, SurfacePatternOptions opts).

root_causes: The QueryExecutor interface was designed around the initial findCollocations use case and extended incrementally with executeSurfacePattern and executeBcqlQuery without normalizing parameter naming
findDependencyCollocations was added to the implementation without a corresponding interface update, suggesting it was intended as a temporary or internal method that was not cleaned up
